 {"id":"gt-0nh8","title":"gt prime should detect mayor role from any rig's mayor/ folder","description":"Currently gt prime only detects the Mayor role when run from town root (~/gt). It should also detect Mayor when run from:
- ~/gt/gastown/mayor/rig (rig's internal mayor dir)
- Any path containing /mayor/ under a rig

This would allow the mayor session to work correctly regardless of which rig directory it's attached to.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T21:56:10.281534-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T21:56:10.281534-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-0odbt","title":"Replace WaitForClaudeReady with gt peek for steady-state agent observation","description":"## Problem

WaitForClaudeReady uses regex to detect Claude's prompt, which is a ZFC violation.

## Architectural Fix

**Bootstrap (ZFC violation acceptable):**
During cold town startup, no AI is available. Regex to get Deacon online is acceptable.

**Steady State (proper ZFC):**
Once any agent is running, AI should observe AI:
- Deacon starting polecats → Deacon uses gt peek
- Deacon restarting → Mayor watches via gt peek
- Mayor restarting → Deacon watches via gt peek

## Implementation

1. Keep WaitForClaudeReady for daemon bootstrap only
2. Update gt deacon trigger-pending to use gt peek
3. Document bootstrap vs steady-state distinction
","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-25T12:34:32.712726-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T12:36:04.186941-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-0pc","title":"Document Overseer role (human operator)","description":"Document the Overseer role in Gas Town architecture.

## The Overseer

The **Overseer** is the human operator of Gas Town. Not an agent - a person.

## Responsibilities

| Area | Overseer Does | Mayor/Agents Do |
|------|---------------|-----------------|
| Strategy | Define project goals | Execute toward goals |
| Priorities | Set priority order | Work in priority order |
| Escalations | Final decision on stuck work | Escalate to Overseer |
| Resources | Provision machines | Use allocated resources |
| Quality | Review & approve swarm output | Produce output |
| Operations | Run gt commands, monitor dashboards | Do the work |

## Key Interactions

### Overseer → Mayor
- Start/stop Mayor sessions
- Direct Mayor via conversation
- Review Mayor recommendations
- Approve cross-rig decisions

### Mayor → Overseer (Escalations)
- Stuck workers after retries
- Resource decisions (add machines, polecats)
- Ambiguous requirements
- Architecture decisions

## Operating Cadence

Typical Overseer workflow:
1. Morning: Check status, review overnight work
2. During day: Monitor, respond to escalations, adjust priorities
3. End of day: Review progress, plan next batch

## Commands for Overseers

```bash
gt status              # Quick health check
gt doctor              # Detailed diagnostics  
gt doctor --fix        # Auto-repair issues
gt inbox               # Messages from agents
gt stop --all          # Emergency halt
```

## Documentation Updates

Add to docs/architecture.md:
- Overseer section under Agent Roles
- Clarify Mayor reports to Overseer
- Add Overseer to workflow diagrams","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:18:03.177633-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}
 {"id":"gt-0pl","title":"Polecat CLAUDE.md: configure auto-approve for bd and gt commands","description":"Polecats get stuck waiting for bash command approval when running
bd and gt commands. Need to configure Claude Code to auto-approve these.

Options:
1. Add allowedTools to polecat CLAUDE.md
2. Configure .claude/settings.json in polecat directory
3. Use --dangerously-skip-permissions flag (not recommended)

Should auto-approve:
- bd (beads commands)
- gt (gastown commands)
- go build/test
- git status/add/commit/push

Should still require approval:
- rm -rf
- Arbitrary commands outside project

Related to polecat prompting (gt-e1y, gt-sd6).","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T14:10:27.611612-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"task"}

 {"id":"gt-8tmz.12","title":"Formula parser and YAML schema","description":"Implement formula parsing from YAML:
- Define YAML schema for .formula.yaml files
- Parse steps, compose rules, vars
- Support extends for formula inheritance
- Validate formula structure

Schema should support:
- formula: name
- description: text
- version: number
- type: workflow|expansion|aspect
- vars: variable definitions
- steps: step definitions
- compose: composition rules

**Beads issue: bd-weu8**","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:20:02.788306-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T13:44:21.239181-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-24T13:44:21.239181-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented in Beads (bd-weu8, bd-wa2l)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.12","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:20:02.788811-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-8tmz.13","title":"bd cook command: Formula to Proto","description":"Implement the 'bd cook' command that transforms formulas into protos.

Usage: bd cook <formula-file> [options]
       bd cook .beads/formulas/*.formula.yaml

Process:
1. Parse formula YAML (uses formula parser)
2. Resolve extends (formula inheritance)
3. Expand macros (expansion rules)
4. Apply aspects (cross-cutting concerns)
5. Generate flat proto bead

Output:
- Creates proto bead in .beads/ with mol-prefix
- Proto has all steps pre-expanded
- Proto is ready for pour/wisp instantiation

**Beads issue: bd-wa2l**","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:20:03.9306-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T13:44:21.26078-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-24T13:44:21.26078-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented in Beads (bd-weu8, bd-wa2l)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.13","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:20:03.933113-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.13","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:20:11.133013-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-8tmz.17","title":"Max expansion depth limit (default 5)","description":"Add maxExpansionDepth config (default 5) to prevent runaway nested expansions. This still allows massive work generation but with a safety bound. Note: distinguish compile-time cooking (produces proto with placeholders) vs runtime cooking (produces mol with real issue IDs). Compile-time is for modeling/estimation/contractor handoff.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:45:09.107895-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T02:23:29.995924-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T02:23:29.995924-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented: DefaultMaxExpansionDepth=5 constant, depth tracking in expandStep, error on limit exceeded","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.17","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:45:09.109857-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.17","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:48:19.037958-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
